title: Starbucks Will Pay Baristas To Create TikTok Content
tldr: Starbucks is paying baristas to make TikTok content, formalizing employee-generated content as a brand strategy. A concrete example of turning frontline staff into an owned media channel.
why_fresh: EGC formalized with pay — a new operational model
angle: When brands put employees on payroll as creators: governance, authenticity and the economics of EGC.

title: A quiet shift in how Americans perceive local news
tldr: Local news long held cross-partisan trust, but new data suggests that advantage is eroding. Signals a meaningful change in media-consumption and trust patterns.
why_fresh: new data on shifting trust trend
angle: What the erosion of local-news trust means for brands relying on regional earned media.

title: The agency keeping creators off the 'influencer cliff'
tldr: A look at how an agency helps creators avoid the sudden collapse of relevance and income. Useful window into the fragility and lifecycle of the creator economy.
angle: The 'influencer cliff': why sustainability, not virality, is the creator economy's real story.

title: Dentsu strikes Meta deal to build plumbing for mass influencer activation
tldr: Dentsu and Meta are building infrastructure for CMOs assembling large creator armies. Signals industrialization of influencer/creator programs at scale.
why_fresh: new infrastructure play for creators at scale
angle: When creator programs need 'plumbing': the operational shift from one-off deals to always-on creator ops.

title: How fandoms are different and why Havas is doubling down on them
tldr: Havas acquired a youth-culture activation agency, betting fandom is distinct from audience and worth building around. A fresh angle on audience psychology and community.
angle: Fandom vs. audience: what shifting from reach to belonging means for content strategy.

title: AI Content Didn't Stop Working, Your Metrics Did
tldr: Argues AI content still performs but old measurement frameworks no longer capture value in an AI-search world. Reframes the attribution and analytics debate.
why_fresh: reframes content measurement in AI era
angle: Why your content isn't failing — your attribution model is, in the age of AI answers.

title: 'Brands recognize the cultural dominance of podcasts': Mel Robbins on AI, ad budgets and audience ownership
tldr: Global podcaster Mel Robbins on why brands treat podcasts as cultural infrastructure and why owning audience matters. Useful creator-side view on audience ownership.
why_fresh: interview, but reinforces known podcast trend
angle: Audience ownership vs. platform rental: what B2B comms teams can steal from top podcasters.

title: How ChatGPT ads are forcing brands to rethink GEO and paid strategy
tldr: ChatGPT introducing ads is pushing brands to rethink generative engine optimization and paid media. Early signal of a new paid+discovery battleground.
why_fresh: emerging GEO/paid shift with ChatGPT ads
angle: GEO becomes a paid channel: how AI-answer advertising reshapes discovery strategy.

title: Why Conair is moving fast to adopt AI-driven video ads
tldr: An A/B test of Amazon's AI video tool cut a Cuisinart video's production time by months — but humans were still needed to finish. Concrete case study on AI-human video workflow.
why_fresh: concrete AI video production case study
angle: Months to minutes, but not zero humans: what the Conair test reveals about realistic AI video workflows.

title: ABC will trial using AI for journalism. What are the risks and benefits?
tldr: Australia's ABC reverses its cautious AI stance, letting staff use Claude in news production via an Anthropic deal. A notable newsroom governance shift.
angle: From caution to Claude: how newsroom AI governance is being rewritten in real time.

title: Who was really at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding? AI slop makes it hard to tell
tldr: Absence of official photos let AI-generated fakes flood the information vacuum around a huge event. Sharp illustration of how synthetic media corrupts consumption.
why_fresh: AI slop filling info vacuums — new dynamic
angle: Information vacuums invite AI slop: why comms teams must fill silence fast.

title: The Information Rebuilds Its App to Own More of the Subscriber's Day
tldr: The Information reworks its app to capture more subscriber time and daily habit. A useful publisher case study on engagement modeling and owned-audience retention.
angle: Owning the subscriber's day: designing content products around habit, not just headlines.

title: Why Febreze is taking on soccer stink via podcasts, experiences
tldr: P&G's 'Can't Wash This' campaign uses podcasts and experiences to ride World Cup/MLS momentum. A concrete campaign breakdown tied to a cultural moment.
why_fresh: solid campaign case but routine tactics
angle: Building a content platform around an unglamorous product: the Febreze soccer play.

title: Summertime poses a threat to your crisis comms plan
tldr: Thin summer calendars and out-of-offices slow crisis decisions from minutes to days, eroding reputation. Practical reminder on crisis-comms readiness.
angle: Seasonal risk gaps: building a crisis-comms plan that survives vacation season.

title: How to Create Content Pillars for LinkedIn (and Why They Make Content So Much Easier)
tldr: A practical framework for using content pillars to make LinkedIn output consistent and easier. Directly useful for executive positioning and personal-brand content.
angle: Content pillars as a personal-brand operating system for busy executives.
